name: Cedar Point
description: Peninsula park on Lake Erie known as "America's Roller Coast" for its dense lineup of record-setting coasters.

name: Six Flags Magic Mountain
description: Southern California coaster park with one of the largest coaster collections in the world.

name: Kings Island
description: Cincinnati-area park home to one of the longest wooden coasters ever built.

name: Universal's Islands of Adventure
description: Themed-land park adjoining Universal Studios Florida, built around immersive IP lands.

name: Disneyland Park
description: The original Disney theme park and birthplace of the modern theme park industry.

name: Six Flags Great Adventure
description: New Jersey park paired with a drive-through safari, known for extreme-height coasters.

name: Busch Gardens Williamsburg

name: Alton Towers
description: UK resort park built around a Gothic revival mansion and its historic gardens.

name: Europa Park
description: Family-owned European-themed park and one of the most visited theme parks in Europe.

name: Silver Dollar City
description: 1880s-frontier-themed park in the Ozarks known for its craftsmanship and coasters.
